clauses.rationale: This clause only identifies the parties and rental unit; none of the candidate rules apply.
The fixed-term dates do not address rent increases, deposits, deposit interest, or pets, so none of the candidate rules apply.
The clause only states the monthly rent amount and payment date; none of the candidate rules concerning deposits or rent increases are implicated.
The clause permits a last-month rent deposit equal to one month's rent and applies it only to the final rental period, consistent with ON-DEPOSIT-CAP and ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause requires a security and damage deposit in addition to the rent deposit, which is prohibited by ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause requires a non-refundable pet deposit, which is expressly prohibited by ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause expressly prohibits all pets and animals in the rental unit and on the property, contrary to ON-NO-PET-BAN.
The clause permits only a refundable key deposit and caps it at the actual replacement cost, which is consistent with ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause violates ON-NO-GUEST-BAN because it requires the landlord’s written permission for overnight guests and imposes an extra guest fee.

The clause permits entry at any time without prior notice for inspections or maintenance, contrary to ON-ENTRY-NOTICE, which generally requires at least 24 hours’ written notice and entry between 8 a.m. and 8 p.m.

The clause concerns the tenant’s responsibility for cleanliness and does not impose a cleaning fee or deposit, restrict subletting, address deposit interest, or authorize landlord entry. None of the candidate rules apply.
The clause assigns responsibility for repairing damage caused by the tenant or guests; it does not ban guests or require any prohibited deposit or fee. None of the candidate rules is violated.
The $75 administration charge for an NSF cheque exceeds the $20 cap under ON-NSF-CAP.
The clause requires the tenant to provide twelve post-dated cheques, contrary to ON-NO-POSTDATED, which permits such payment only when chosen voluntarily.

The clause permits rent increases at any time with only 30 days’ notice, contrary to ON-RENT-INCREASE-NOTICE, which requires at least 90 days’ written notice and generally limits increases to once every 12 months.
The clause states that no interest is payable on any deposit, contrary to ON-DEPOSIT-INTEREST, which requires annual interest on the rent deposit at the rent increase guideline rate.
The clause merely makes contents insurance optional and does not concern post-dated payments, sublet consent, prohibited deposits, or interest on a rent deposit. None of the candidate rules apply.
The clause imposes an absolute ban on assignment or subletting, contrary to ON-SUBLET-CONSENT, which permits these with the landlord's consent and prohibits unreasonable withholding of consent.
The clause concerns the landlord’s repair and maintenance obligations and does not conflict with any of the candidate rules.
The clause violates ON-VITAL-SERVICES because it permits the landlord to shut off electricity, heat, or water due to unpaid rent.

The clause allows the landlord to change the locks without providing new keys to the tenant, contrary to ON-LOCK-CHANGE. Conditioning replacement keys on payment of rent arrears does not comply with that rule.
The clause concerns smoking and does not address pets, subletting, guests, or landlord entry, so none of the candidate rules apply.

The clause only requires the tenant to establish and pay their electricity account directly; it does not authorize the landlord to withhold or interfere with electricity, and the other candidate rules do not apply.
This severability clause does not prohibit pets, interfere with vital services, restrict subletting, or impose a rent increase, so none of the candidate rules apply.
